text: Robust Multi-Object Tracking (MOT) necessitates a sophisticated understanding of the complex spatial and temporal interactions between targets. In this paper, we introduce TransMOT, a novel framework that utilizes spatial-temporal graph transformers to model these object dependencies effectively. By representing target trajectories as a series of sparse weighted graphs and integrating spatial graph transformer encoders, temporal transformer encoders, and spatial graph transformer decoders, TransMOT achieves superior computational efficiency and tracking accuracy compared to standard Transformer architectures. To further enhance performance, we propose a cascade association framework designed to manage low-confidence detections and mitigate long-term occlusions, which are traditionally resource-intensive for transformer models. Evaluated across several challenging benchmarks, including MOT15, MOT16, MOT17, and MOT20, TransMOT consistently achieves state-of-the-art results, demonstrating its robustness in diverse and crowded environments.
